data.title: Ats Corporation Balances Growth With Strategic Reset
data.description:
Ats Corporation ((TSE:ATS)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Ats Corporation’s latest earnings call painted a cautiously optimistic picture, as management balanced solid operational gains with clear acknowledgment of near-term headwinds. Executives highlighted double-digit full-year growth, expanding margins, stronger cash flow and leverage metrics, and a growing backlog anchored in regulated markets. Yet they also flagged weaker Q4 bookings, restructuring charges, and a modest growth outlook as reasons for measured expectations.

Full-Year Revenue and Adjusted Earnings Growth

Ats reported that full-year revenue and adjusted earnings from operations grew approximately 11% year-over-year, underscoring broad-based execution across its diversified platform. Management framed this performance as evidence that prior investments in scale, processes, and portfolio focus are now translating into consistent top- and bottom-line gains.

Fourth Quarter Revenue and Earnings Increase

In the fourth quarter, adjusted revenues reached $744 million, up 3.2% from a year earlier, with organic growth of 1.5% and a 1.7% tailwind from foreign exchange. Adjusted earnings from operations climbed 3.4% to $76.8 million, while adjusted EPS came in at $0.36, reinforcing the narrative of steady, if not spectacular, progress.

Strong Backlog and Life Sciences Positioning

The company closed the fiscal year with an order backlog of roughly $2.0 billion, a key indicator of revenue visibility in coming periods. Life Sciences accounted for $1.1 billion, or 55% of that backlog, and management pointed to a broader funnel spanning radiopharma, mail-order pharmacy, lab automation, and other high-value applications.

Energy Backlog Momentum

Energy was another bright spot, with backlog rising about 40% year-over-year on the strength of nuclear refurbishment, life-extension, and new build programs. Ats also noted ongoing engagement with small modular reactor developers, suggesting a pipeline of opportunities in a sector where long-duration, regulated projects can underpin future growth.

Gross Margin and Services Mix Improvement

Fourth quarter gross margin improved to 29.4% of adjusted revenues, a 36 basis point increase versus the prior year. Management credited this expansion largely to a higher mix of services and spare parts, which carry better margins than project-based work and are central to the company’s efforts to build a more resilient earnings profile.

Working Capital and Cash Flow Progress

Non-cash working capital improved to 12.1% of revenues, marking the third straight quarter of improvement and bringing it comfortably below the 15% target. Operating cash flow in Q4 reached $149.5 million, and executives emphasized multi-quarter progress in free cash flow as a key enabler of balance sheet strength and capital flexibility.

Leverage Reduction and Capital Allocation Flexibility

Ats lowered its net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio to 2.8x, the fourth consecutive quarter of improvement and within the targeted 2.0 to 3.0 times range. With CapEx and intangible investment totaling $76.7 million in the year and fiscal 2027 CapEx guided to $70–90 million, management argued it now has room to fund growth, pursue selective M&A, and manage risk without overextending the balance sheet.

Clear Margin and Strategic Targets

Looking ahead, the company aims to exit fiscal 2027 with adjusted earnings from operations margins 50–75 basis points higher than fiscal 2026 levels. It also reaffirmed a longer-term target of 15% adjusted earnings from operations margin, positioning this goal as a product of mix shift, recurring revenue growth, and ongoing efficiency initiatives.

Aftermarket and Recurring Revenue Focus

Roughly one-third of Ats’s revenue currently qualifies as recurring, reflecting services, spare parts, and other ongoing offerings. Management is embedding aftermarket operations within business units and targeting further expansion of recurring revenue, viewing it as a critical driver of margin predictability and improved earnings resilience.

Order Bookings Decline

Despite the robust backlog, Q4 order bookings fell to $704 million, an 18.4% decline year-over-year, partly due to a tough comparison against several large enterprise orders in the prior period. The trailing 12-month book-to-bill ratio stood at 0.99 to 1, indicating that bookings slightly lagged revenue conversion and raising questions about future backlog growth if this trend persists.

Transportation Repositioning and Revenue Step-Down

Ats is deliberately moving away from large-scale automotive projects in its transportation segment, citing their dilutive impact on profitability. This repositioning will remove about $50 million of revenue and transportation will no longer be reported as a standalone vertical, creating a short-term revenue headwind but aiming to enhance the company’s margin profile over time.

Significant Restructuring and One-Time Costs

The fourth quarter included several sizable charges tied to restructuring and portfolio realignment, including $28.3 million related to transportation repositioning and $9.8 million for embedding services into business units. An additional $15.2 million of restructuring costs was recorded, and management expects around $5 million more in the first quarter plus another $5–10 million elsewhere during fiscal 2027.

Moderation and Lumpiness in Life Sciences

While Life Sciences remains Ats’s largest backlog contributor, management noted that orders have normalized after a strong fiscal 2025 period driven in part by GLP-1-related auto-injector demand. That specific portion, previously around 20% of the Life Sciences backlog, has moderated, introducing some timing-related lumpiness even as radiopharma and adjacent applications show encouraging momentum.

Modest Revenue Growth Outlook for Fiscal 2027

The revenue outlook for fiscal 2027 is described as modest, reflecting both normalization in demand patterns and the intentional transportation step-down. For the first quarter, management guided revenue in the range of $700–$740 million, signaling a pause in more aggressive growth while the company digests restructuring and focuses on higher-quality, margin-accretive work.

Higher SG&A and Stock-Based Compensation Run-Rate

Selling, general, and administrative expenses excluding adjustments rose to $139.5 million in Q4, up $5.6 million year-over-year, driven mainly by foreign exchange and higher professional fees. Stock-based compensation was $2.4 million in the quarter, but management expects this to normalize at about $5 million per quarter going forward, implying a somewhat higher ongoing cost base.

Book-to-Bill Slightly Below One

The trailing 12-month book-to-bill ratio of 0.99 to 1 underscores that new orders are roughly matching, but not yet outpacing, revenue recognition. While current backlog levels provide a solid cushion, sustained sub-1.0 book-to-bill could constrain backlog growth over time, making future booking trends a key metric for investors to monitor.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Strategic Direction

Management’s guidance calls for first-quarter revenue between $700 million and $740 million and modest top-line growth for fiscal 2027, including the impact of the transportation revenue step-down. The company aims to enhance margins by 50–75 basis points by the end of fiscal 2027, maintain CapEx within $70–90 million, and continue sharpening its focus on regulated markets like Life Sciences, Energy, and Food & Beverage, which now make up about 80% of backlog.

Ats’s earnings call presented a company in transition but firmly on the front foot, leaning into higher-margin markets, recurring revenue, and tighter capital discipline. While near-term growth is modest and bookings softness plus restructuring costs pose risks, the improved margin trajectory, strong regulated-market backlog, and healthier balance sheet suggest a deliberate shift toward quality over quantity that investors will watch closely.

data.title: H2O America announces executive Kristen Johnson’s upcoming departure
data.description:

H2O America ( (HTO) ) has issued an announcement.

On June 10, 2026, H2O America disclosed that it had reached a mutual agreement with executive Kristen Johnson for her to separate from the company, with her departure becoming effective July 3, 2026. The company classified the move as a separation without cause under her employment agreement and committed to pay her the contractually defined severance, signaling an orderly, non-contentious management transition for stakeholders.

The severance package will be paid in accordance with the terms of a severance agreement tied to Johnson’s existing contract, indicating that H2O America is adhering to its executive employment commitments. This structured approach to the leadership change aims to minimize disruption to operations and governance while providing clarity on the financial and organizational impact of Johnson’s exit.

data.title: Caro Holdings Acquires Minority Stake in Tanzanian Mining Assets
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Caro Holdings ( (CAHO) ) just unveiled an update.

On June 9, 2026, Caro Holdings Inc. entered into an Asset Purchase and Acquisition Agreement with Ontario-based Goldrange Resources Corp. to acquire a 49% undivided interest in Goldrange’s rights to certain mining properties located in Tanzania, Africa. As consideration, Caro Holdings will issue 20,000,000 shares of its common stock to Goldrange, with the deal governed by customary representations, warranties, covenants, indemnities and termination provisions.

The share issuance will be conducted as a private transaction exempt from registration under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, relying on Section 4(a)(2), and the new shares will carry restrictive legends limiting transfer. The transaction marks a significant step in Caro Holdings’ expansion into African mining assets and alters its capital structure, with potential implications for existing shareholders through equity dilution and increased exposure to international resource markets.

More about Caro Holdings

Caro Holdings Inc. is a publicly traded company that issues common stock with a par value of $0.00001 per share and is expanding its presence in the mining sector. The company is seeking exposure to mining assets in Africa through strategic equity-financed acquisitions, indicating a focus on resource-based growth and international asset diversification.

data.title: Genprex Faces Nasdaq Delisting Notice, Plans Compliance Steps
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Genprex ( (GNPX) ) has issued an update.

On June 10, 2026, Genprex received a Nasdaq notice that its shares had closed below the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days, rendering it non-compliant with Nasdaq’s continued listing rules and ineligible for a standard compliance period due to a prior reverse stock split on October 21, 2025. Nasdaq staff therefore moved to delist the company’s securities, prompting Genprex to state it will promptly request a hearing, which stays any suspension or delisting action in the near term.

Genprex said it plans to pursue measures to regain compliance with the $1.00 bid requirement, potentially including another reverse stock split, but warned there is no assurance the hearing panel will grant continued listing or that any price recovery would be durable. The company cautioned that delisting could materially reduce its share price, impair liquidity and market capitalization, and significantly hinder its ability to raise capital and maintain investor and partner confidence, posing broader risks to its business and stakeholders.

More about Genprex

Genprex, Inc. is a publicly traded biotechnology company whose common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company develops and seeks to commercialize clinical-stage product candidates, requiring ongoing access to capital markets and a stable stock listing to support its research, manufacturing and commercialization activities.

Average Trading Volume: 615,548

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $6.92M

data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Opendoor Technologies ( (OPEN) ) has provided an update.

Opendoor Technologies Inc. held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on June 11, 2026, with about 65.45% of outstanding shares represented, and stockholders elected David Benson, Eric Feder, and Eric Wu as Class III directors to serve three-year terms ending at the 2029 annual meeting. Shareholders also ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as the independent auditor for the 2026 fiscal year and approved, on a non-binding basis, the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, signaling continued support for current governance, oversight, and executive pay practices.

These voting outcomes indicate broad shareholder backing for Opendoor’s board composition and external audit arrangements, which may provide stability for the company’s strategic and financial oversight. The advisory approval of executive compensation, despite notable opposition votes, suggests investors are generally aligned with management’s pay structure while still closely scrutinizing leadership performance and incentives.

data.title: Outfront Media Issues New Senior Unsecured Notes Offering
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Outfront Media ( (OUT) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On June 12, 2026, OUTFRONT Media Inc. and its financing subsidiaries issued $500 million of 6.000% senior unsecured notes due June 15, 2034 in a private placement exempt from U.S. securities registration. The notes, guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by the company and certain subsidiaries, pay interest semiannually starting December 15, 2026 and are governed by an indenture with customary high-yield-style covenants and events of default.

The indenture restricts additional indebtedness, dividends, certain investments, asset sales, liens, affiliate transactions, prepayments of specified debt, and subsidiary stock issuances, with some covenants falling away if the notes achieve and retain investment-grade ratings from Moody’s and S&P. The company has multiple redemption options, including make-whole and equity clawback features before 2029 and scheduled call prices thereafter, and must offer to repurchase the notes at 101% upon a defined change of control, shaping its future capital structure flexibility and creditor protections.

The most recent analyst rating on (OUT) stock is a Buy
with a $38.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Outfront Media stock,
see the OUT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OUT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OUT is a Neutral.

The score is mainly held back by balance-sheet leverage despite improving fundamentals. The latest earnings call was a clear positive with accelerating growth and higher expected AFFO, while technicals are moderately supportive but not strongly trending. Valuation is a modest headwind due to a higher P/E, partially offset by the dividend yield.

The company finances its capital needs and supports ongoing operations through a mix of debt and equity, including senior notes issued via its financing subsidiaries in U.S. private markets.

data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.

An announcement from BioRestorative Therapies ( (BRTX) ) is now available.

On June 10, 2026, BioRestorative Therapies entered into a revolving loan agreement with Bowery Group LLC, giving the company the ability to draw up to $1 million for general corporate purposes under a 12% annual interest rate, with a one-year maturity for each loan and customary default provisions. The facility is expected to bolster the company’s liquidity and financial flexibility, but also introduces higher-cost debt that stakeholders will weigh against its potential to support ongoing operations.

Effective June 11, 2026, directors Francisco Silva, Nickolay Kukekov and Patrick F. Williams resigned pursuant to the loan agreement, and on June 12, 2026 the board appointed three lender designees — Mika Grasso, Katharyn Field and Jatinder Dhaliwal — as independent directors with key committee roles. These board changes deepen capital markets and governance expertise on the board and signal increased influence by the lender over BioRestorative’s strategic oversight and corporate governance structure.

An announcement from SmartKem ( (SMTK) ) is now available.

On June 12, 2026, SmartKem, Inc. disclosed that the directors of its wholly owned U.K. subsidiary, SmartKem, Ltd., have instructed an administrator to commence a creditors’ voluntary liquidation process for the subsidiary. The company emphasized that this action at the subsidiary level will not affect SmartKem, Inc.’s current operations, and it has made no bankruptcy or reorganization filings in either the U.S. or the U.K., signaling continuity for its main business and stakeholders.

The announcement, signed by Chief Financial Officer Barbra C. Keck on June 12, 2026, underscores that SmartKem, Inc. remains operational despite the liquidation move for SmartKem, Ltd. This distinction suggests that creditors’ claims and restructuring efforts will be contained within the subsidiary, aiming to limit disruption to the parent company’s day-to-day activities and its broader market positioning.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SMTK is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by persistent losses and ongoing cash burn with elevated financing/dilution risk. Technicals show short-term strength but are overbought and still weak versus the 200-day trend. More about SmartKem

data.title: Wellgistics Regains Nasdaq Bid Compliance Amid Equity Risks
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest update is out from Wellgistics Health Inc ( (WGRX) ).

Wellgistics Health Inc. disclosed that on April 13, 2026, Nasdaq notified the company that its stockholders’ equity, reported at negative $12.45 million as of December 31, 2025, failed to meet the $2.5 million minimum required for continued listing. After outlining plans to combine with DataVault AI and acquire assets including Tollo Health, Nasdaq granted Wellgistics an extension until October 12, 2026, to demonstrate compliance via specified reporting alternatives, while warning that failure to show sufficient equity by the 2026 annual filing could trigger delisting proceedings.

Separately, the company reported that a December 10, 2025 Nasdaq notice had found it out of compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price rule, but that this issue was resolved when the stock traded at or above $1.00 from May 26 to June 8, 2026. Nasdaq confirmed on June 9, 2026 that Wellgistics had regained bid-price compliance and that its shares remain listed and traded on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the symbol “WGRX,” though the company continues to face equity-compliance risk if its planned transactions do not materialize as anticipated.

Spark’s Take on WGRX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WGRX is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by very weak financial performance (steep revenue decline, deeply negative margins, negative equity, and sustained cash burn). Technicals also reflect a clear downtrend with negative momentum. Valuation provides limited support because the company is loss-making (negative P/E) and has no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on WGRX stock,
click here.

More about Wellgistics Health Inc

Wellgistics Health Inc. operates in the healthcare sector and is listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker “WGRX.” The company is pursuing a strategic combination with DataVault AI to form DelivMeds AI and plans to acquire an intellectual patent portfolio and a controlling stake in Tollo Health, targeting a post-transaction stockholders’ equity of about $40 million and a combined asset value of roughly $4 billion.

data.title: Shake Shack Adds Retail Veteran Christiane Pendarvis to Board
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An update from Shake Shack ( (SHAK) ) is now available.

On June 12, 2026, Shake Shack announced it will expand its board from eight to nine members and appoint retail executive Christiane Pendarvis as a director, effective July 2, 2026. Pendarvis, deemed an independent director, will serve as a Class II board member, join the Nominating & Corporate Governance Committee and stand for election at the company’s 2029 annual shareholder meeting.

Pendarvis brings more than 25 years of experience in direct-to-consumer and omnichannel retail, including leadership roles at PATTERN Beauty, Savage X Fenty, Victoria’s Secret, Aerie, Old Navy and other consumer brands, as well as board service at Hootsuite and other organizations. Her appointment underscores Shake Shack’s effort to bolster strategic expertise in digital commerce, brand building and scaling operations, reinforcing its growth ambitions and potentially strengthening its competitive position and stakeholder value as the company expands its global footprint.

More about Shake Shack

Shake Shack Inc. is a fast-casual restaurant chain known for elevated versions of American classics, including made-to-order Angus beef burgers, crispy chicken, hand-spun milkshakes, house-made lemonades, beer and wine. Founded in 2004 in New York City’s Madison Square Park, the company has grown into a global cult brand with more than 695 locations worldwide, emphasizing high-quality ingredients, hospitality and community-focused operations.

The chain operates over 450 restaurants across 35 U.S. states and the District of Columbia, alongside more than 245 international outlets in major cities such as London, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Singapore, Mexico City, Istanbul, Dubai, Tokyo and Seoul. Shake Shack also leverages digital channels, including its mobile Shack App, to enhance guest convenience and support its broader strategy of deepening customer engagement across both physical and online platforms.

data.title: Centessa Shareholders Approve Eli Lilly Acquisition Scheme
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An update from Centessa Pharmaceuticals ( (CNTA) ) is now available.

On June 12, 2026, Centessa Pharmaceuticals’ shareholders overwhelmingly approved the court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement under the U.K. Companies Act through a Scheme Meeting, with 126,653,456 votes in favor and only 23,007 against, paving the way for Eli Lilly subsidiary LDH XV Corporation to acquire all issued and to be issued Centessa shares. At a concurrent general meeting, investors also authorized the board to implement the scheme and amend the company’s articles, while the 2026 Annual General Meeting saw all resolutions passed, including the reappointment of directors and KPMG as auditor and the adoption of 2025 accounts without a dividend, underscoring broad shareholder support ahead of a U.K. court hearing scheduled for June 22, 2026 to sanction the transaction.

Centessa reported strong shareholder turnout at both the Scheme Meeting and Company General Meeting, with 126,676,463 ordinary shares represented out of 154,731,309 eligible, satisfying quorum requirements and reinforcing legitimacy of the approval process. At the Annual General Meeting held the same day, 134,569,251 shares were present or represented by proxy, and shareholders backed all ordinary resolutions, including advisory approval of the 2025 directors’ remuneration report, signaling confidence in the company’s governance as it transitions toward acquisition by Eli Lilly outside the scope of the U.K. Takeover Code.

The most recent analyst rating on (CNTA) stock is a Hold
with a $42.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Centessa Pharmaceuticals stock,

data.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT Issues June 2026 Portfolio Update
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest announcement is out from ARMOUR Residential REIT ( (ARR) ).

On June 12, 2026, ARMOUR Residential REIT released a June 2026 update detailing its portfolio and capital structure as of May 31, 2026, with CPR data as of June 4, 2026, showing a $22.2 billion portfolio heavily concentrated in agency mortgage‑backed securities, notably 30‑year fixed‑rate pools, and a modest allocation to U.S. Treasury positions and TBA exposures. The company reported a debt‑to‑equity ratio of 7.9, implied leverage of 8.0, liquidity of about $1.16 billion equal to 46% of total capital, significant reliance on repurchase financing from affiliate BUCKLER Securities, and a June 2026 monthly dividend of $0.24 per common share implying a 16.8% yield, underscoring its high‑leverage, high‑yield profile and extensive interest‑rate hedging via $15.3 billion notional in swaps.

ARMOUR’s June 2026 update highlights its heavy concentration in agency MBS and 30‑year fixed pools, with a total portfolio around $22.2 billion and small TBA and Treasury positions. The REIT also emphasizes high leverage and liquidity, significant repo funding from BUCKLER, and a rich 16.8% dividend yield supported by a large interest‑rate swaps book.

ARMOUR’s portfolio data for May 2026 indicate a $22.2 billion book dominated by agency MBS, especially 30‑year fixed‑rate conventional pools, plus limited TBA and Treasury exposure. Key metrics show high leverage, substantial liquidity as a share of capital, and continued emphasis on a high‑yield monthly dividend, reflecting a strategy geared toward income generation underpinned by active hedging and repo financing from multiple counterparties including BUCKLER.

Zeo ScientifiX ( (ZEOX) ) has shared an update.

On June 10, 2026, Zeo ScientifiX granted fully vested stock options for 625,000 shares each to principal stockholder and consultant Greyt Ventures LLC, CEO and CFO Ian Bothwell, and Chief Medical Officer George Shapiro, all exercisable on a cashless basis over ten years at $1.67 per share. The company also granted 625,000 performance-based incentive options each to the same three parties with anti-dilution provisions tied to future large equity issuances, and awarded a separate fully vested option for 80,000 shares to non-executive director Chuck Bretz with a five-year term, collectively reinforcing long-term alignment between leadership and shareholders while potentially affecting future share structure.

These grants centralize substantial equity incentives among key insiders, including a principal stockholder and senior executives, which may influence governance dynamics and the company’s capital structure over time. The anti-dilutive terms attached to the performance-based options could limit the dilutive impact of future large share issuances on these option holders, shaping how Zeo ScientifiX structures subsequent financing or strategic transactions involving significant new equity.
